checks.evidence: robots.txt returns HTTP 200 with valid directives including User-Agent rules for ia_archiver, GPTBot, OAI-SearchBot, ChatGPT-User, and a wildcard section.

checks.description: robots.txt does not block important pages
checks.evidence: Disallow rules block app-specific paths only: /new?*, /chat/*, /share/*, /magic-link*, /api/*, /onboarding*, /upgrade*, /lti/*, /settings*, /task*. These are all application routes, not public content pages. The root (/) is not blocked for the wildcard user-agent.

Sitemap is referenced in robots.txt (Sitemap: https://claude.ai/sitemap.xml) but the URL returns a Cloudflare challenge page (403 via curl, challenge page content instead of XML). Sitemap is not accessible to crawlers.
checks.fix: Ensure sitemap.xml is accessible without triggering Cloudflare challenge. Whitelist the sitemap path from bot protection or serve it as a static file.

checks.evidence: claude.ai is behind Cloudflare bot protection. All curl requests to internal paths return 403 (challenge page). Cannot verify link status codes via automated crawl. The site is a Single Page Application — most internal links are app routes (chat, projects, artifacts) that require authentication.
http://claude.ai returns 301 redirect to https://claude.ai/. HTTPS loads successfully in browser.
Viewport meta tag present: width=device-width,initial-scale=1,viewport-fit=cover
Lighthouse Core Web Vitals: LCP = 21,745ms (poor, threshold <2,500ms), CLS = 0 (good, threshold <0.1), FID/Max Potential FID = 669ms (poor, threshold <100ms). LCP and FID both fail.
checks.fix: LCP of 21.7s is extremely poor. Investigate heavy JavaScript bundles blocking render. The SPA architecture loads all app code before paint. Consider server-side rendering for the initial landing page, code splitting, and deferring non-critical JS. Reduce main-thread blocking to fix FID.

checks.evidence: No <link rel='canonical'> tag found on the homepage (https://claude.ai/new). The page lacks a canonical URL entirely.
checks.fix: Add <link rel='canonical' href='https://claude.ai/'> to the homepage. Each publicly accessible page should have a self-referencing canonical tag.

checks.evidence: Sitemap URL returns Cloudflare challenge HTML instead of valid XML. No <url> entries can be parsed.
checks.fix: Serve sitemap.xml as a static file that bypasses Cloudflare challenge. Ensure it contains valid XML with absolute URLs for all public pages.

checks.description: No duplicate titles or meta descriptions
checks.evidence: Only one page could be crawled (homepage). The title is generic: 'Claude' (6 characters). As a SPA, all routes likely share the same initial HTML title. Cannot verify cross-page duplicates due to app architecture.
checks.fix: Implement dynamic title tags per route. Each public-facing page should have a unique, descriptive title (50-60 characters). Current title 'Claude' is too short and non-descriptive.

checks.evidence: Lighthouse Time to Interactive: 25,880ms (25.9 seconds). Far exceeds the 3-second threshold.
checks.fix: TTI of 25.9s indicates massive JavaScript payload. Implement code splitting, lazy loading of non-critical modules, and consider a lighter landing page that doesn't load the full chat application.

checks.evidence: https://claude.ai/llms.txt returns HTTP 200 via curl but serves the SPA shell HTML, not an actual llms.txt file. The SPA catches all routes and renders the app. No dedicated llms.txt content exists.
checks.fix: Create a dedicated /llms.txt file served as static text content, bypassing the SPA router. Include guidance for AI models about Claude's purpose and usage policies.

checks.evidence: Lighthouse performance score: 29/100.
checks.fix: Performance score of 29 is extremely low. Address LCP (21.7s), reduce JavaScript bundle size, implement code splitting, and optimise the critical rendering path.

checks.evidence: Lighthouse accessibility score: 97/100.

Non-existent URLs return HTTP 403 (Cloudflare challenge) via curl. In-browser, non-existent routes are caught by the SPA router and redirect to /new (the app). No proper 404 status code or error page is served.
checks.fix: Implement server-side 404 handling for unknown routes. Return HTTP 404 status code with a user-friendly error page instead of redirecting to the app.

checks.evidence: No robots meta tag with noindex found on the homepage. The meta robots attribute is null.
Cannot inspect HTTP response headers via Playwright browser automation. Cloudflare challenge prevents curl-based header inspection.

The homepage has minimal content (app interface only — chat input box). Total words on page: very low. The site is a SPA so all content is dynamically loaded. Not a traditional content page, but search engines may interpret the thin initial HTML as a soft 404.
checks.fix: Consider adding server-side rendered content for the landing page that provides meaningful text content about Claude for search engines.

checks.evidence: No canonical tag present on the homepage. The page cannot self-reference without one.
checks.fix: Add <link rel='canonical' href='https://claude.ai/'> to the homepage.

checks.evidence: No canonical tag present, so no target to check.
Sitemap XML is not accessible (returns Cloudflare challenge). Cannot verify any sitemap URLs.
checks.fix: Fix sitemap accessibility first (see TECH-C3), then ensure all listed URLs return HTTP 200.

checks.evidence: 1 JSON-LD block found on the homepage containing an @graph with WebSite and Organization types.
JSON-LD block parses successfully as valid JSON. No parse errors.
Homepage has WebSite and Organization types, which are appropriate for the homepage of a web application.

checks.description: Author fields use Person or Organization
checks.evidence: No author fields present in the structured data. WebSite and Organization types do not require author fields.

checks.description: Required fields present per schema type
checks.evidence: WebSite: has name ('Claude') and url ('https://claude.com') — required fields present. Organization: has name ('Anthropic') and url ('https://www.anthropic.com') — required fields present. However, WebSite url points to 'https://claude.com' (not claude.ai), which may be intentional (domain redirect) but could confuse crawlers if claude.com doesn't resolve or redirect.
checks.fix: Verify that https://claude.com resolves correctly and redirects to claude.ai. If claude.com is the canonical domain, consider using it consistently. If not, change the WebSite url to https://claude.ai.

checks.evidence: Organization schema present with name ('Anthropic'), url ('https://www.anthropic.com'), logo, and sameAs social links.
Only the homepage was crawlable. claude.ai is a SPA where all routes are app-internal. No non-homepage public content pages exist to evaluate.
No blog or article pages exist on claude.ai. The site is a web application, not a content site.
No FAQ content detected on claude.ai. The site is a chat application interface.

WebSite: has name, url, alternateName. Missing: description, publisher. Organization: has name, url, logo, sameAs. Missing: description, contactPoint, address, foundingDate. Coverage: ~50% of recommended fields.
checks.fix: Add 'description' to WebSite schema. Add 'description' and 'contactPoint' to Organization schema to improve rich result eligibility.

checks.evidence: No product pages exist on claude.ai.
Homepage uses @graph with 2 schema types: WebSite and Organization. Multiple relevant types provide richer signals.
Organization schema includes sameAs with 3 social profiles: x.com/AnthropicAI, linkedin.com/company/anthropic, youtube.com/@anthropic-ai.
